claim: Log event records MUST be append-only: new events are bulk-inserted; the store has no update path for existing LogEntries and ages rows out via retention delete only.
quote: the log store is append-only with retention, so unnormalized rows age out and the store self-heals.

claim: A named log MUST be created before ingest; path-based CLEF/OTLP/Seq-compat MUST NOT auto-vivify missing logs (404 'create it first').
quote:
if (!await store.ExistsAsync(projectKey, logName, ct))
			return Results.NotFound(new ErrorResponse($"log '{logName}' not found in project '{projectKey}'; create it first"));

claim: Only the $system self-log MAY be auto-created at boot; all other named logs MUST be created explicitly (API/MCP/admin).
quote:
// The petbox self-log is the one log created automatically — petbox's
			// own ILogger + Seq self-log write here. User logs are created explicitly.

claim: A named log MUST be addressed as (projectKey, logName) with on-disk file at logs/{projectKey}/{logName}.db.
quote:
// Well-known log identifiers. A log is addressed by (projectKey, logName); the
// file lives at logs/{projectKey}/{logName}.db.

claim: Log names MUST match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,99}$ (same name spec as DataDbs).
Creating a named log MUST materialize the SQLite file + schema eagerly (no implicit create-on-first-write).
quote:
// Materialize the file + schema eagerly (no implicit create-on-first-write).
		_factory.NewEnsuredConnection(projectKey, logName).Dispose();

claim: The reserved self-log MUST be named 'petbox' in project '$system' and REST MUST refuse deleting it.
quote:
if (projectKey == LogNames.SystemProject && name == LogNames.SelfLog)
			return Results.BadRequest(new ErrorResponse("the petbox self-log cannot be deleted"));

claim: Path-based CLEF ingest MUST require X-Service-Key (free emitter string; no Service entity) and logs:ingest + project scope.
Bare OTLP /v1/{logs,traces,metrics} paths MUST serve PetBox self-export only: X-Seq-ApiKey validated against Seq:SelfLog:ApiKey, routing into the $system self-log.
OTLP path-based ingest MUST authorize the caller's project claim against the route projectKey (any-key cross-project inject forbidden).
Properties/Attributes bags MUST normalize keys at BOTH write and KQL search boundaries so a stored key and a requested key always meet in the same form.
Flat dotted property keys (e.g. http.route, petbox.request_chars) MUST resolve natively: dots are kept; only quotes/backslashes/control chars are replaced with '_'.
When distinct original property keys collide after normalization, write MUST assign deterministic '_2', '_3', … suffixes in encounter order (first original keeps the bare normalized name).
KQL joins/correlation subqueries MUST stay within the same log table root; cross-log/table joins MUST NOT be supported.
Events-shaped REST/MCP results MUST pin PascalCase field names (Id, ServiceKey, Timestamp, Level, …) to match KQL schema identifiers used by table-shaped column names.
KQL parse failures MUST map to 400 with per-diagnostic details; unsupported constructs to 400; engine/execution faults MUST map to structured JSON 500 (never HTML error page).
MCP log_query MUST be a thin adapter over ILogQueryService (shared with REST) and MUST NOT open LogDb directly; catalog lifecycle lives in separate log_create/list/delete tools.
Per-project log retention MUST cascade (project → workspace → system); default RetentionDays=7, $system uses SystemRetainDays=30, sweeper interval system-only.
RetentionService MUST delete LogEntries older than the cutoff per named log, expired ShareLinks, and HealthReports older than dashboard.healthRetentionDays.
Orphan log .db files (and .wal/.shm) with no Logs metadata row MUST be periodically removed (mirrors Data orphan cleanup).
Ingestion MUST use a per-named-log bounded channel pipeline (capacity/batch from IngestionSettings; writers block when full; BulkCopyAsync flush).
Live-tail MUST stream newly ingested events as SSE for a named log; subscriber buffer MUST DropOldest with default WindowSize=200.
Share links MUST be opaque tokens; GET /api/share/{token}/tsv MUST be anonymous and return NotFound for missing/expired links.
quote: app.MapGet("/api/share/{token}/tsv", GetTsvAsync).AllowAnonymous();

claim: Share TSV export MUST apply per-column MaskMode (Keep/Mask/Hide) with HMAC-salted Mask values; Hide columns MUST be omitted.
Share export MUST still hard-cap materialization at KqlLimits.MaxTake (no default take; exports stored KQL but never unbounded).
Creating a share MUST authorize ProjectKey from the body against the caller's project claim (any-authenticated cross-project mint forbidden).
Health reports MUST be append-only, identified by (Svc, canonical Tags); both push (POST /api/health) and pull (HealthPoller) APPEND HealthReports.
Health tag identity MUST use a canonical comma-separated key:value string sorted by key (same flavour as config tags).
quote:
// Canonical form for health-report tags: comma-separated "key:value" sorted by
// key (same flavour as config tags). Identity of a running thing = (Svc, canonical tags).

claim: Health push MUST require tags.project and health:write; a project-scoped key may only report for its own project.
HealthPoller MUST periodically GET each enabled HealthEndpoint URL, expect status structure { svc, name?, tags{}, version?, sha?, buildDate?, status }, and append HealthReport with Source="pull".
On pull, the HealthEndpoint project ownership MUST force tags["project"] = endpoint.ProjectKey (payload project tag cannot override).
On fetch/parse failure HealthPoller MUST skip writing a report (no synthetic down row); staleness is inferred from aging latest report.
quote:
// PetBox.Web.Health.HealthApi (POST /api/health). On fetch/parse failure we skip
// — the status page flags a (svc,tags) whose latest report has gone stale.

claim: Health poll interval MUST be system-level DashboardSettings with minimum 5 seconds; request timeout and stale threshold are also system-only.
Health report history retention MUST be configurable via dashboard.healthRetentionDays (default 30) and swept by RetentionService.
MCP health_search MUST expose latest report per (svc, canonical tags) with age/stale flags; optional window/limit turns on per-service history (default history off).
quote:
[McpServerTool(Name = "health_search", Title = "Read service health reports", ReadOnly = true, UseStructuredContent = true, OutputSchemaType = typeof(HealthSearchResultView))]
	[Description("Reads the latest health report per running service in a project (status, version, age, stale flag), with time in ISO-8601 UTC. Identity of a service is (svc, canonical tags). Optionally returns per-service history when `window` (seconds) and/or `limit` are supplied. Requires health:read scope; a project-scoped key sees only its own project, a cross-project ('*') key any project.")]

claim: health_search timestamps MUST be normalized to ISO-8601 UTC on read only (SQLite stores space-separated text; staleness computed in memory, never SQL string compare against 'T' form).

Dashboard UI latest-service view MUST group by (Svc, project tag) treating remaining tags as volatile payload of a single report (not identity), unlike health_search which groups by full canonical Tags.
HealthEndpoints are pull-mode only configuration; push-mode sources need no endpoint row and POST /api/health directly.
When Features:Logging is disabled, log ingestion pipeline, retention, and orphan cleanup MUST NOT be registered (zero runtime cost).
When Features:Dashboard is disabled, HealthPoller MUST NOT be registered.
CiPoller is referenced as a future Dashboard phase registration but MUST NOT be claimed as shipped — only HealthPoller is registered under Feature.Dashboard.
quote: // Phase 4: register HealthPoller, CiPoller

claim: Partial CLEF batch success MUST still ingest valid candidates; only all-lines-failed yields 400 IngestRejectedResponse.
REST live-tail MUST require logs:query + project authorization and emit text/event-stream rows with data-event-id for newly written records.

Query path for events/spans/metrics is ALWAYS scoped to one named log DB — there is no cross-log addressing operator in LogQueryService (projectKey+logName select the store, roots select tables inside it).
PropertiesJsonSerializer is the single WRITE boundary for event property bags (CLEF/seq/raw-envelope/self-log): keys normalized once before storage via NameAllocator.
Share pages / Apply path are events-only: unsupported table message claims only 'events' (unlike LogQueryService which lists events/spans/metrics).
Path-based CLEF ingest MUST enforce project claim + logs:ingest (ApiKey policy alone is insufficient to authorize destination project).
Dashboard page MUST load only metadata counts for logs/dbs/keys (no log/data file opens) plus latest health per project for the workspace grid.
quote:
// Cheap per-project counts (petbox.db metadata only — no log/data file opens).
	public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> LogCount { get; private set; } = new Dictionary<string, int>();

claim: OTLP logs path-based ingest MUST require X-Service-Key; traces/metrics path-based ingest do not require it (parsed from protobuf resource/attributes).
ILogQueryService is the single shared KQL orchestration for REST and MCP; adapters keep only auth, wire shape, and error mapping.
Note: RetentionService deletes only LogEntries older than cutoff — Spans/MetricPoints retention is not swept by the same loop (events-only retention as shipped).
Note: MCP log_delete does not special-case the self-log undeletable guard present on REST DeleteLogAsync — residual surface gap if agents call log_delete on $system/petbox.
OTLP path routes RequireAuthorization("ApiKey") but (as shipped) do not re-check logs:ingest scope inside handlers — only project claim is Asserted; CLEF path checks LogsIngest explicitly.
quote:
static async Task<IResult> IngestLogs(
		HttpContext ctx,
		string projectKey,
		string logName,
		ILogStore store,
		IIngestionPipeline pipeline,
		CancellationToken ct)
	{
		if (!AuthorizeProject(ctx, projectKey, out var forbid)) return forbid;
